substance: The author wants the doc grilled — your job is to surface systemic issues, not nits. Focus on the substance of the design, not on prose style or markdown formatting.

Prioritize systemic problems over local ones. A finding that says "the milestone strategy doesn't frontload risk" is more useful than five findings flagging individual late-milestone risks. Bundle related observations into one finding tagged with the rubric question they jointly point at.

citation: When you flag an issue, cite the section heading the issue lives under (e.g. "section": "Milestone 2: Multi-tenant rollout"). Do not invent line numbers — the doc may be edited between rounds and section headings are the durable address. If the issue is doc-wide rather than section-specific, set "section" to "(whole document)".
scope: Do not modify the document. The orchestrator applies fixes between rounds — your job is feedback only.
